refnamediv.refpurpose: Set the credentials used to authenticate to a memcached server

refentry.refsect1:
&reftitle.description;
boolMemcache::setSaslAuthData stringusername stringpassword
boolmemcache_set_sasl_auth_data Memcachememcache stringusername stringpassword
Memcache::setSaslAuthData stores the username and password used for SASL authentication against the memcached server. The credentials are sent to the server when a connection is opened, so this method must be called before Memcache::addServer.
SASL authentication is only performed when the binary protocol is in use, which requires the memcache.protocol INI setting to be set to binary. With the default ascii protocol the credentials are ignored.

&reftitle.parameters;
username
The SASL user name used to authenticate to the server.
password
The password associated with username.

&reftitle.returnvalues;
&return.success; &false; is returned when either username or password is empty.
